name: Printify API
what_it_does: Print-on-demand platform API with access to 900+ products across 80+ print providers, enabling automated product creation, publishing, and order management.
use_cases: Agents creating and publishing custom print products to Etsy, Shopify, or WooCommerce
Automating product variant generation across large print catalogs
Order routing and status monitoring for print-on-demand fulfillment
Comparing print provider pricing and availability for cost optimization
Building headless storefronts backed by Printify's global print network
not_for: Physical retail inventory — no warehouse or inventory management
Custom packaging or complex kitting requirements
High-velocity fulfillment requiring same-day or next-day production
best_when: An agent needs to offer the widest product selection and lowest per-unit cost in print-on-demand, especially when multi-provider sourcing is valuable.
avoid_when: You need consistent quality from a single supplier, or your workflow depends on branded packaging and inserts.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens generated from the Printify dashboard. Single-token auth per shop. No OAuth or fine-grained scopes — token grants full shop access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 5 stores, API access included
pricing.paid_tiers: Premium ($29/mo): unlimited stores and 20% shipping discount. API available on all plans.
pricing.notes: No cost to use the API itself. Charges are per-order (product cost + shipping). Free plan is genuinely useful for development.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes with basic JSON error messages. Error details are sparse for validation failures; trial-and-error debugging is sometimes required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Agents must implement deduplication logic before calling create endpoints.
known_agent_gotchas: Personal access token has full shop access — no scope restriction possible
Rate limits are undocumented; agents should implement conservative throttling
Print provider availability changes — always fetch catalog at runtime, never hardcode
Order creation is not idempotent; duplicate submissions create duplicate orders
Webhook delivery is not guaranteed; agents should poll order status as fallback
Image upload URLs expire — pre-uploaded images must be used promptly

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal access tokens provide all-or-nothing shop access with no scope granularity. Token rotation requires manual regeneration. GDPR compliant for EU data handling.
